_note: Representative SAMPLE in the shape of onyx-dot-app/onyx_deep_research_bench, used to seed a FlowEvaluationDataset for measuring the Deep Research workflow. This is a small starter slice, NOT the full benchmark — replace/extend by importing the real benchmark from the upstream repo. Each item: {id, question, reference?}.

questions.question: What are the main architectural differences between retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and long-context LLMs, and when is each preferable?
questions.reference: Should compare retrieval (external index, cite-able, scalable corpus, staleness handled by re-index) vs long-context (no retrieval step, limited by window/cost, better for self-contained docs). Mentions hybrid approaches and cost/latency trade-offs.

questions.question: Summarize the current state of self-hosted vector databases suitable for production RAG, with trade-offs.
questions.reference: Covers pgvector, Qdrant, Weaviate, Milvus, Vespa; trade-offs on scale, hybrid search, ops burden, filtering, HNSW/IVF indexing.

questions.question: How do feature-flag platforms support progressive delivery, and what are the risks of percentage rollouts?
questions.reference: Targeting rules, % rollout, kill switches, deploy/release decoupling; risks: sticky bucketing, sample bias, observability gaps, flag debt.

questions.question: Compare SearXNG, Serper, and Brave Search APIs for programmatic web search in an agent pipeline.
questions.reference: SearXNG self-hosted/free/meta-search; Serper/Brave hosted APIs with keys, rate limits, cost; quality, freshness, ToS considerations.

questions.question: What are the key approaches to document-level permission enforcement in enterprise RAG?
questions.reference: ACL sync from source systems, per-chunk permission metadata, query-time filtering by user/group, late-binding vs index-time, SCIM/group sync.

questions.question: Outline a benchmarking methodology for evaluating multi-step deep-research agents.
questions.reference: Reference reports, LLM-as-judge with rubrics, citation accuracy, factuality, coverage/recall of sub-questions, reproducibility, leaderboard.
